text: During a laparoscopic pariotic lymph node dissection for rectal cancer, the surgeon tents the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and identifies a lymph node at the IMA root. The dissection proceeds as the IMA root is separated from the aorta, followed by transection of the IMA.

The surgeon carefully dissects the para-aortic lymph nodes, which are densely attached to the aortic wall. An engorged vein is visible on the lateral side of the para-aortic lymph node, extending from the pelvis to the level of the kidney, with a branch leading to the left kidney. The lymph node is dissected from the aorta, left ureter, and the engorged venous structure, and is removed en bloc. Another engorged venous structure is observed behind the aorta.
The surgeon removes the rectal cancer specimen, which includes a bulky para-aortic lymph node, and performs intracorporeal anastomosis to reconnect the bowel.
